title: Chicken Karahi

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Karahi Recipe (Pakistani Style)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Pakistani cooking with our authentic Chicken Karahi recipe. Learn to make this flavorful, spicy chicken dish step-by-step at home. Perfect for dinner!
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant pot of authentic Pakistani Chicken Karahi, garnished with fresh ginger and green chilies, ready to serve.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich, spicy flavors of Pakistan with our easy-to-follow Chicken Karahi recipe – perfect for your next family meal!

description: Chicken Karahi is a quintessential Pakistani dish, known for its rich, spicy tomato-based gravy and tender chicken pieces. Cooked in a traditional karahi or wok, it boasts a vibrant flavor profile from fresh ginger, garlic, green chilies, and aromatic spices. This hearty and flavorful curry is perfect for sharing with family and friends, offering a true taste of authentic Pakistani cuisine.

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat oil in a large karahi or wok over high heat. Add the chicken pieces and fry until they are lightly golden brown and sealed. Remove the chicken from the karahi and set aside, leaving the oil in the pan.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tomato Base
instructions.text: In the same oil, add the ginger-garlic paste and saute for about 1 minute until fragrant. Add the chopped tomatoes and cook over medium-high heat, stirring occasionally, until they soften and start to break down, about 8-10 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Spices and Cook
instructions.text: Once the tomatoes are soft, mash them with the back of your spoon. Add red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Mix well and cook the spices for 2-3 minutes until the oil separates from the masala, stirring continuously to prevent burning.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Simmer
instructions.text: Return the seared chicken to the karahi. Add the whisked plain yogurt and slit green chilies. Mix everything thoroughly. Cover the karahi and reduce the heat to low. Let it simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the chicken is tender and cooked through.

instructions.title: Intensify Flavors
instructions.text: Uncover the karahi and increase the heat to medium-high. Cook, stirring occasionally, until any excess liquid evaporates and the oil separates from the gravy. This step is crucial for developing the rich, concentrated flavor of the karahi. Stir in the garam masala powder.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Remove the karahi from heat. Garnish generously with freshly julienned ginger and chopped fresh coriander. Serve your authentic Chicken Karahi hot with fresh naan, roti, or plain steamed rice for a truly delightful Pakistani meal.

tips: To achieve the best flavor, always cook Karahi on relatively high heat initially to sear the chicken and develop a rich base before simmering.
For an extra kick, you can add a pinch of crushed black pepper or a few extra green chilies towards the end of cooking.
Chicken Karahi is best served immediately. If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2-3 days and reheat gently on the stovetop.
article: Chicken Karahi is a beloved Pakistani Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
